description: PTPN22 rs2476601 (R620W, C1858T) variant affecting lymphocyte phosphatase function. The T allele increases T-cell activation, predisposing to autoimmune conditions including type 1 diabetes (~2.5x risk in TT homozygotes). Also associated with rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, and thyroiditis.

source.title: A functional variant of lymphoid tyrosine phosphatase is associated with type I diabetes
source.authors: Bottini N, Musso T, Nieto M, et al.

AA.phenotype: High genetic susceptibility to type 1 diabetes and other autoimmune conditions
AA.effect: High genetic susceptibility to type 1 diabetes and other autoimmune conditions
AA.recommendation: Family screening recommended if T1D diagnosis. Genetic counseling appropriate. Monitor for signs of T1D (thirst, polyuria, weight loss). Annual screening with HbA1c and fasting glucose in at-risk relatives. Consider also screening for other autoimmune conditions (thyroiditis, RA, SLE).

AG.phenotype: Moderately increased genetic risk for type 1 diabetes and autoimmune conditions
AG.effect: Moderately increased genetic risk for type 1 diabetes and autoimmune conditions
AG.recommendation: Standard T1D screening for symptomatic relatives. Counsel on early warning signs. Annual monitoring of metabolic markers if family history of T1D.

GA.phenotype: Moderately increased genetic risk for type 1 diabetes and autoimmune conditions
GA.effect: Moderately increased genetic risk for type 1 diabetes and autoimmune conditions
GA.recommendation: Standard T1D screening for symptomatic relatives. Counsel on early warning signs. Annual monitoring of metabolic markers if family history of T1D.

GG.phenotype: No increased PTPN22-related T1D genetic risk
GG.effect: No increased PTPN22-related T1D genetic risk
